context: Planning a trip runs on a few time words: is it open today, can we go tomorrow, was that yesterday? You don't need to tell the time in Spanish - you need today, tomorrow, and the days of the week so you can book and not turn up on a closed day.
explanation: Begin with the three that anchor everything: "hoy" (today), "mañana" (tomorrow) and "ayer" (yesterday). Heads-up: "mañana" also means "morning" - which one is meant is always clear from context.
